title: FHIR ImplementationGuide + LinkML-generated docs starter-kit
context: The M3 'shipped' deliverable requires a published FHIR ImplementationGuide (IG) and a docs starter-kit that an adopter can use to understand and instantiate the standards. The IG is generated from the mCODE/FHIR crosswalk profiles using the HL7 FHIR IG Publisher/Sushi; the docs site is generated from the LinkML CDE source. Both must lead with the standards-not-a-data-store and not-medical-advice notices and render the CDE set, crosswalks, and consent/governance templates with provenance. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-crosswalk-001 and rare-cancer-registry-templates-consent-001. Standards reviewer and maintainer sign-off required.
objective: Build the FHIR ImplementationGuide (`ig/`) and the docs starter-kit (`docs/`) that: (1) publish the rare-cancer CDE FHIR profiles generated from the mCODE crosswalk, (2) render the CDE set, vocab bindings, and crosswalks with provenance sourced from the LinkML output, (3) include the consent/governance templates, (4) provide an adopter onboarding guide explaining how to run a consent-first registry under the adopter's own IRB/REC and custody, and (5) lead every page with the standards-not-a-data-store and not-medical-advice notices.
acceptanceCriteria: FHIR IG is generated and validates via the HL7 FHIR IG Publisher from the mCODE-aligned crosswalk profiles.
Published IG and docs site lead with 'standards-not-a-data-store' and 'not medical advice / not legal advice' notices on every page.
The CDE set, crosswalks, and consent/governance templates are rendered with provenance (source standard, identifier, licence note) per the prov-001 model.
Adopter onboarding guide explains how to instantiate a consent-first registry under the adopter's IRB/REC and custody, making clear that Elyos holds nothing.
`pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green; standards reviewer and maintainer sign-off recorded.
output: An `ig/` FHIR ImplementationGuide and a `docs/` starter-kit site generated from LinkML and the FHIR IG Publisher, committed via PR with standards reviewer and maintainer sign-off.
